description: Select LLM as the job type in the fine-tuning wizard
steps: Navigate to the fine-tuning wizard in Solo Hub (Solo Gym section)
On Step 1, click the 'LLM' button (with the brain icon)
explanation: Choosing LLM unlocks text-generation fine-tuning with LoRA. Available on all plans including Basic. VLA requires Plus/Pro.
expected_outcome: LLM is selected; LLM model list and LLM-specific params become visible in subsequent steps

validation.rule: LLM button is highlighted in orange; LLM model list is visible on the same wizard screen

explanation: These are the base models you're fine-tuning on top of. Llama-3.1-8B is recommended for most use cases.

validation.rule: Model name is highlighted/selected in the LLM model list

description: Select a GPU for the training job

available_gpus.name: H100 80GB
available_gpus.status: Recommended — fastest, lowest total credit burn

available_gpus.name: A10 24GB
available_gpus.status: Insufficient VRAM — do not use for LLM fine-tuning

explanation: Faster GPUs finish sooner and reduce total credit burn. H100 is recommended unless you have specific VRAM requirements. Credits are deducted per minute — a faster GPU means fewer minutes.

validation.rule: GPU card is highlighted; it shows 'Available' or 'Recommended' badge (not 'Insufficient VRAM')

common_errors: Selecting A10 24GB — marked as Insufficient VRAM; job will fail at provisioning

epochs.description: Number of full passes over the dataset

max_steps.description: Maximum training steps; overrides epochs if reached first

lora_rank.description: LoRA rank — higher rank = more capacity, more VRAM

lora_alpha.description: LoRA alpha — set to 2× rank for a safe starting point (e.g. Rank 16 → Alpha 32)

gradient_accumulation.description: Accumulate N gradients before updating — effectively increases batch size

explanation: All values are slider-driven with recommended ranges shown. LoRA is the fine-tuning method — it adapts the base model without retraining all weights.
expected_outcome: All 6 parameters set; proceed to Step 4

validation.rule: All parameter sliders have values within their valid ranges

description: Connect the HuggingFace training dataset for an LLM job

dataset_repo_id.description: The HuggingFace dataset repo ID. Must exist and be accessible.

text_field.description: The column name in your dataset that contains training text. This is the most common cause of LLM job failures — it must EXACTLY match the column name in your dataset.
text_field.detail: If your dataset has a 'messages' column with list-of-turn objects, use 'messages' — the trainer applies the model's chat template automatically. If your dataset uses instruction/response or question/answer columns, leave Text Field as 'messages' (or any non-present column name) to use the instruction-response pairing path instead.

chat_messages.description: Preferred format. A 'messages' column where each row is a list of turns. Trainer applies the model's chat template automatically.

instruction_response.description: Alternative: paired columns like instruction+response or question+answer.
instruction_response.text_field_tip: When using this format, leave Text Field as 'messages' (or any column name that does NOT exist in the dataset) so training uses the paired-column path

example_datasets_from_solo.description: Code-style reasoning tasks for instruction fine-tuning
Math-style reasoning tasks with step-by-step solutions

expected_outcome: Dataset connected; wizard ready for Step 5

validation.rule: Dataset repo ID and Text Field filled in; no red validation error shown

critical_warning: A wrong Text Field value (column name mismatch) is the #1 cause of LLM job failures. Double-check it against the actual column name in your HuggingFace dataset before launching.

description: Optionally configure HuggingFace Hub push and W&B tracking

hf_token.description: Your HuggingFace API token with write access

repository_name.description: HF repo to push the trained model to

push_to_hf_hub.description: Pushes the trained model weights to HuggingFace Hub after training completes. Optional — model is always saved to your Solo Hub org regardless.

weights_and_biases.description: Streams training metrics (loss, steps, runtime) to your W&B project for visualization.

explanation: Both integrations are optional. Trained models are always uploaded to your Solo Hub org by default. These extend where results go.
expected_outcome: Integrations configured or skipped; wizard ready for Step 6

validation.rule: If enabled — HF token and repo name fields filled; if W&B enabled — API token filled. If disabled — toggles are off.

common_errors: Invalid HF token — verify token has write access in your HuggingFace account settings
Wrong repo name format — must be username/model-name (with slash), not just a name
W&B project doesn't exist — create it in W&B first before launching

description: Review cost estimate and launch the training job

cost_estimate_fields.max_runtime: How long the job can run at current balance (e.g. ~104 hrs)

explanation: Credits are deducted per minute starting from Provisioning stage. The job auto-stops when balance reaches zero. There is no manual cap to set — your balance IS the cap.
steps: Review the cost estimate panel carefully
Confirm you have enough credits for your expected training duration
expected_outcome: Job submitted. You see a results card with Job ID, model name, and initial status QUEUED.

validation.rule: Results card appears showing Job ID, Output Model Name, and pipeline status = QUEUED. A direct link to the model's detail page is shown.

common_errors: Start Training button disabled — you may lack fine-tuning permissions; on Basic with VLA selected (will revert to LLM); or account/org issue
Insufficient credits — add credits or upgrade plan before launching
Job already exists — output model name must be unique within org; choose a different name
Maximum concurrent jobs reached — wait for an existing job to reach Completed/Failed/Cancelled
